notes: WIN-FND-004 (reduced-motion half). This pins the pre-existing, general LibreOffice accessibility reduced-motion primitive (predates this fork; tdf#115688/tdf#161765 lineage) as the mandatory foundation any future native Material motion role must route through, per docs/design/01-foundations.md section 5. It is deliberately thin and does NOT satisfy this row's Material-motion (SRC/M) gate: the trip-wire keeps that gate visibly open by failing closed the moment a native motion/duration/easing token appears in definition.xml. Native Material duration/easing token roles, the lo-pop entrance, and their VclBuilder/weld wiring do not exist in source and are the separate build gates. Source evidence only: runtime_verified is false.

declarations.note: The four MiscSettings reduced-motion / allow-animation declarations must still exist.

checkpoints.note: GetUseReducedAnimation delegates to the platform frame rather than returning a hardcoded value.
Each IsAnimated*Allowed reads its officecfg Accessibility::AllowAnimated* key.

contiguous.note: The Windows backend reads SPI_GETCLIENTAREAANIMATION and returns its negation, as a contiguous pair.

repeated.note: On the 'System' (0) case each of the three IsAnimated*Allowed functions negates GetUseReducedAnimation(), so the OS preference is respected once per function.

schema.note: The three accessibility animation toggles keep xs:short type and default value 0 ('System' = use the OS setting).

material_flag.note: Stage 1 of the Material rewrite adds a stored MaterialReducedMotion boolean (Appearance group, default false), surfaced by the Options > Appearance 'materialtheme' checkbox. It is honest-inert: no native Material motion role consumes it yet. Its EFFECTIVE value is the OR of this flag with MiscSettings::GetUseReducedAnimation() (the pre-existing chain pinned above), realized only when a native Material motion primitive lands (Stage 3+). Pinning the stored key here keeps it from silently gaining a live effect without revisiting this contract; the definition.xml trip-wire stays the SRC gate.

trip_wire.note: definition.xml must carry zero motion/duration/easing token elements or attributes. This fails closed the moment native Material motion tokens land, forcing this contract (and the row's SRC gate) to be revisited rather than silently satisfied.
